title: Buy VOO and MAG7 only on red trading days—better than DCA
author: Odd_Pepper7149
selftext: Over the past year and a half, my dollar-cost averaging strategy has changed slightly. I no longer invest daily, but primarily when VOO opens lower. My portfolio consists of a core group of ETFs and stocks (VOO, QQQ, and Mag 7). Whenever I decide to buy, I prioritize buying the holdings that have fallen the most that day (by percentage), while buying less (or not at all) of holdings that have risen or surged. For example, I bought 5 shares of META on Friday. This opportunistic strategy allows me to buy quality stocks in the S&P 500 at relatively lower prices when the market is weak. I expect this strategy to deliver slightly higher long-term returns than a passive VOO single-stock strategy because it systematically improves the cost-base efficiency of high-conviction stocks when prices fall. Of course, my predictions may not be accurate, as I don't have over a decade of stock market experience. Moreover, the market is highly volatile these days, and no one can predict what tomorrow will bring. If this trend continues, I expect this strategy to work.
